title: Repository Baseline, Go 1.26 Toolchain, Modules, and Reproduction · Mastery Check
summary: A release reviewer compares near-miss Go decisions for repository baseline, go 1.26 toolchain, modules, and reproduction and accepts only runnable changed-case evidence, explicit non-claims, and named browser, compiler, race, network, filesystem, terminal, load, accessibility, and production transfer gates. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for reproducible Go Pokedex repository baseline. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice,…
objectives: Inspect revision, branch, worktree, ignored files, configuration, tests, fixtures, secrets, licenses, and recovery anchor before editing.
Record Go 1.26.5, GOOS, GOARCH, GOTOOLCHAIN, module mode, build tags, and supported platform matrix.
Choose module path, command and internal package boundaries, dependency direction, and import visibility before growth.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

content.text: A quiz Go team handles case 2629 during a race-instrumented cache stress case. Build deterministic Go evidence to reconcile Go toolchain, module, clean-build, target, and dependency evidence; browser code must remain deterministic and side-effect-free; then change one resource identity, pagination link, JSON field, status, or body budget and revalidate admission. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original fixtures only. Real PokéAPI traffic, DNS, sockets, TLS, host files, terminals, signals, race and leak claims, load, cross-platform packaging, accessibility review, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. For Pokedex product engineering and Go Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Pokedex Go Clean Reproducti…, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: A binary built from a dirty developer cache is reproducible evidence.
